lines: Welcome to rest. [long pause] There's nothing you need to do right now... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] nothing you need to figure out... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] nothing you need to fix or finish. [long pause] Just be where you are.

Take a moment to adjust your position. [long pause] Find the way your body wants to lie tonight. [long pause] Maybe shift your shoulders a little... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] rock your hips from side to side until they settle... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] let your head find its place on the pillow. [long pause] There's no perfect position. [long pause] There's just the one where your body says... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] okay... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] I can stop adjusting now.

Now notice where your body meets the bed. [long pause] Start with the back of your head... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] feel the weight of your skull resting down. [long pause] Your shoulder blades... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two broad points pressing gently into the mattress. [long pause] The curve of your lower back. [long pause] The heaviness of your hips. [long pause] The backs of your thighs. [long pause] The calves. [long pause] The heels of your feet.

Each of these points of contact is a place where you can stop holding yourself up. [long pause] The bed is doing that work. [long pause] It's been doing it all along. [long pause] You've just been too alert to notice. [long pause] Notice it now. [long pause] Let the bed hold you completely.

Bring your attention to your breathing. [long pause] Don't change it yet... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] just notice the rhythm that's already there. [long pause] The quiet rise of the chest... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the gentle fall. [long pause] The way the body breathes itself, whether you pay attention or not. [long pause] It's been doing this all day without you thinking about it. [long pause] It will keep going tonight.

Now, gently begin to lengthen your exhale. [long pause] Breathe in through your nose for a count of four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] one... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four. [long pause] And out through your mouth, slowly, for eight... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] one... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] seven... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] eight. [long pause] Feel the breath thin out at the end, like a thread of smoke dissolving.

Again. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four. [long pause] And out... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] long and slow... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] letting the breath carry everything you don't need out of your body... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] six... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] seven... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] eight.

One more. [long pause] In... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] filling the lungs gently... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and out... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] completely... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] all the way to empty. [long pause] Good. [long pause] Let your breathing find its own pace now. [long pause] You've set it in motion. [long pause] It knows what to do.

Tonight, we're going to walk through the most beloved psalm ever written. [long pause] Twenty-three. [long pause] And as we walk through it, we'll let it do what it was written to do... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] quiet us down... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] settle us into the care of God... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and lead us, gently, toward sleep.
"The Lord is my shepherd. [long pause] I shall not want."

Let those words rest in you. [long pause] Not as something to analyze or theologize about... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] but as a statement about tonight. [long pause] Right now, in this bed, in this room, with whatever is on your mind... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] you are watched over. [long pause] You are tended to. [long pause] You lack nothing that you need for this exact moment. [long pause] The moment of lying down.
"He maketh me to lie down in green pastures."

He maketh you to lie down. [long pause] Not suggests. [long pause] Not offers. [long pause] Not asks politely. [long pause] He maketh you. [long pause] There is a firmness to this kindness. [long pause] A shepherd knows the sheep won't rest on their own. [long pause] They'll keep grazing, keep moving, keep watching for threats, even when they're exhausted. [long pause] So the shepherd leads them to a place where lying down is the only thing left to do. [long pause] That's where you are tonight. [long pause] You have permission to stop striving. [long pause] More than permission. [long pause] You are being led to rest by someone who knows you need it more than you do.
And so we begin there. [long pause] Lying down. [long pause] Letting the body release, group by group, the way a field releases the heat of the day after the sun goes down.

Bring your attention to your feet. [long pause] Gently curl your toes... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] squeeze the muscles in both feet, the arches, the soles... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] hold them tight for five seconds. [long pause] One... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five. [long pause] And release. [long pause] Let them go completely. [long pause] Feel the muscles unknot... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] feel the warmth that floods in where the tension was. [long pause] Your feet resting on cool, thick grass. [long pause] The kind of grass that gives beneath you... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] soft and damp at the roots.

Now your calves. [long pause] Tighten the muscles in both calves... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] pull your toes up toward your shins to engage them fully. [long pause] Hold that tension. [long pause] One... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five. [long pause] And let go. [long pause] Feel them soften and spread against the mattress. [long pause] Heavy and warm... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] like they're resting in sun-warmed grass, the blades soft against your skin, the earth solid and cool beneath.

Your thighs. [long pause] Squeeze the muscles in both thighs... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] press your legs down firmly into the bed. [long pause] Feel the tension run from your knees to your hips. [long pause] Hold. [long pause] One... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five. [long pause] And release. [long pause] Feel the heaviness pour down through your legs like warm water. [long pause] All the standing and walking and climbing of the day... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] all that effort... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] draining into the mattress, draining into the earth beneath the green pastures.

Move to your hips and your lower back. [long pause] Gently press your lower back into the mattress... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] tighten your glutes... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] feel the whole base of your body engage. [long pause] Hold. [long pause] One... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five. [long pause] And let go. [long pause] Feel your pelvis settle... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] your lower back widen and flatten against the bed. [long pause] The earth beneath you, ancient and patient, holding your weight without complaint.

Your stomach and your chest. [long pause] Take a deep breath in and tighten your abdomen... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] pull everything in toward your spine, squeeze your ribs together. [long pause] Hold it. [long pause] One... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five. [long pause] And release with a long exhale. [long pause] Feel your belly soften completely. [long pause] Feel your ribs open and expand outward. [long pause] There's space here now... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] space to breathe deeply... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] space to let the lungs do their easy, automatic work.

Now your hands. [long pause] Make two fists. [long pause] Squeeze them tight... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] fingernails pressing into your palms, tendons standing out along the backs of your hands. [long pause] Hold. [long pause] One... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five. [long pause] And open them. [long pause] Let your fingers uncurl slowly, one by one... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] let your palms face upward or downward, wherever they fall. [long pause] Feel the blood rushing back into your fingers, a gentle tingling, a warmth spreading through your open hands. [long pause] Your hands have nothing to grip tonight. [long pause] Nothing to type, nothing to carry, nothing to hold onto.

Your forearms and your upper arms. [long pause] Bend your elbows and flex both arms, tensing everything from wrist to shoulder. [long pause] Hold it all tight. [long pause] One... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five. [long pause] And drop them. [long pause] Let your arms fall heavy at your sides like they're made of warm clay. [long pause] Feel them sink into the bed... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] warm... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] loose... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] finished with effort for the day.

Your shoulders. [long pause] This is where the day hides. [long pause] This is where you've been carrying things you didn't even know you were carrying. [long pause] Lift your shoulders up toward your ears... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] high as they'll go... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] feel the tension bunch at the top. [long pause] Hold them there. [long pause] One... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five. [long pause] And let them fall. [long pause] Not lower them... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] let them fall. [long pause] Feel the distance between your ears and your shoulders grow. [long pause] Feel the neck lengthen. [long pause] That's the release. [long pause] That's the day leaving your body.

And finally, your face. [long pause] Scrunch everything up... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] forehead, eyes, nose, mouth, jaw... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] squeeze your whole face toward the center, as tight as you can. [long pause] Hold it. [long pause] One... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] two... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] three... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] four... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] five. [long pause] And let go. [long pause] Feel your forehead smooth out like water after a stone drops in. [long pause] Your eyelids soften. [long pause] Your jaw drops slightly open. [long pause] The muscles around your eyes release their grip. [long pause] Your tongue settles at the bottom of your mouth, loose and warm. [long pause] Your cheeks rest. [long pause] Your lips part gently.

Your whole body now... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] from the soles of your feet to the crown of your head... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] has been invited to let go. [long pause] And it has accepted. [long pause] You are lying down in green pastures. [long pause] This is what the psalm describes. [long pause] Not a metaphor tonight... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a physical reality. [long pause] Your body is at rest in a place of safety.
"He leadeth me beside the still waters."

See it now. [long pause] Let the picture form slowly, the way an image develops in a dark room. [long pause] A stream. [long pause] Barely moving. [long pause] The water so calm that the surface reflects the sky above it like a mirror. [long pause] Silver and pale gold in the fading light.

The stream is narrow enough that you could step across it. [long pause] The banks are low, covered in thick grass and clover. [long pause] The water moves over smooth stones on the bottom... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] stones that have been here longer than anyone can remember... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] worn round and flat by centuries of patient current. [long pause] You can hear it... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a sound so quiet you have to stop everything else to catch it. [long pause] The soft, continuous murmur of water over stone.

You're sitting beside this stream, though sitting is generous... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] you're more lying than sitting now, propped on one elbow, then giving in to the slope of the bank and lying back. [long pause] The grass beneath you is dense and cool, the kind that tickles the backs of your arms. [long pause] Each blade distinct. [long pause] The earth underneath is firm but gives slightly, like it's making room for you.

The air has that quality it gets in the last hour before dark. [long pause] Still, but not stagnant. [long pause] Cool on your skin but not cold. [long pause] It carries scent... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] green things growing, the mineral smell of clean water, wild herbs at the water's edge. [long pause] Mint, maybe. [long pause] Or something like thyme... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] earthy and clean. [long pause] You breathe it in and it goes deep, not just into your lungs but into some part of you that needed exactly this.

The light is changing. [long pause] The gold is deepening toward amber, then copper. [long pause] The shadows of the trees along the far bank stretch long across the water. [long pause] A single bird calls from somewhere behind you... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a clear, unhurried note... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and then settles into silence. [long pause] Even the bird is done with the day.

Listen to the water. [long pause] That's all. [long pause] Just the water and the silence around it. [long pause] The stream doesn't hurry. [long pause] It doesn't worry about where it's going. [long pause] It just moves, steady and sure, toward whatever comes next.

With each breath, feel yourself being restored. [long pause] Not by your own effort... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] not by willpower or discipline or positive thinking. [long pause] By presence. [long pause] The way a field restores itself when you stop plowing it. [long pause] The way water clears when you stop stirring it. [long pause] The way a wound heals when you stop touching it. [long pause] You don't have to fix anything tonight. [long pause] You are being restored by the One who made you... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] who knows how you work... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] who designed rest for exactly this purpose.
"Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil, for thou art with me."

Whatever shadows followed you today... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] whatever worry or regret or unnamed dread came to bed with you tonight... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] hear this. [long pause] The psalm does not say there are no valleys. [long pause] It does not say the shadows aren't real. [long pause] It does not promise that you will never walk through dark places. [long pause] It says something more honest and more powerful than that. [long pause] It says... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] you are not alone in them. [long pause] You are accompanied through the dark. [long pause] That is a different thing entirely from pretending the dark is not there.
"Thy rod and thy staff, they comfort me."

The rod to protect. [long pause] The staff to guide. [long pause] A shepherd's tools, designed not to punish but to keep the sheep from wandering into danger. [long pause] Feel that comfort now. [long pause] Not as an abstract idea... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] but as something close. [long pause] Like a hand resting on your shoulder in a dark room. [long pause] Like a voice nearby that doesn't need to say much... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] just... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] I am here. [long pause] I know the way through this valley. [long pause] Stay close. [long pause] You can close your eyes. [long pause] I'll watch.
"Thou preparest a table before me in the presence of mine enemies."

Even now... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] even with whatever troubles are out there circling... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] a table has been set. [long pause] There is provision. [long pause] There is enough. [long pause] Not because the enemies have gone away... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] but because the host is greater. [long pause] You can eat at a table set in the open, in full view of everything that threatens, because the One who set it is not worried about what's watching.
"Thou anointest my head with oil. [long pause] My cup runneth over."

Abundance. [long pause] Not scarcity. [long pause] Not barely enough. [long pause] More than enough. [long pause] The cup overflows. [long pause] Tonight, you are not running on empty. [long pause] You are being filled.
"Surely goodness and mercy shall follow me all the days of my life."

Not chase you. [long pause] Follow you. [long pause] Goodness and mercy are not running you down. [long pause] They are walking behind you, quiet and steady... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] guarding the path you've already traveled... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] covering the missteps... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] filling in the low places. [long pause] Every day you've lived, they were there. [long pause] Every day to come, they will be. [long pause] They don't depend on your performance. [long pause] They follow because that is their nature.
"And I will dwell in the house of the Lord... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] forever."

Lord... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] thank you for this night. [long pause] Thank you for the green pastures you lead us to, even when we resist the resting. [long pause] Thank you for the still waters that ask nothing of us. [long pause] Quiet what is loud in us tonight. [long pause] Restore what the day has worn down. [long pause] We trust you with the hours we can't see through. [long pause] Amen.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

You are lying in green pastures now. [long pause] The stream is beside you... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] its sound so steady it has become part of the silence, the way a heartbeat becomes part of sleep. [long pause] The air is cool on your skin. [long pause] The grass is soft beneath your body. [long pause] The sky above you has turned deep blue, then deeper, and now the first stars are appearing... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] faint, then clear, then bright.

The light is almost gone. [long pause] Everything is blue and quiet and still. [long pause] Your body is heavy against the earth. [long pause] Your breathing is slow and even. [long pause] The shepherd is near. [long pause] You can't see him... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] but you don't need to. [long pause] You can feel his presence the way you feel the ground beneath you... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] constant, unhurried, sure.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

The water moves over the stones. [long pause] The grass holds you. [long pause] Your eyes are closed. [long pause] Your hands are open. [long pause] Goodness is behind you. [long pause] Mercy is beside you. [long pause] The shepherd watches.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

Green and quiet... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the water... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the grass... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the cool air... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] the stars appearing one by one.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]

The pasture holds you. [long pause] Sleep comes the way night comes... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] not all at once... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] but gently... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and then completely.
[long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] [long pause]
